questionHtml:
Dinitrogen monoxide in the stratosphere is converted to nitrogen monoxide, NO (g).

Write two equations to show how NO (g) catalyses the decomposition of ozone.
markschemeHtml:
NO (g) + O3 (g) → NO2 (g) + O2 (g)       [✔]
NO2 (g) + O3 (g) → NO (g) + 2O2 (g)     [✔]

 

Note: Ignore radical signs.

Accept equilibrium arrows.

Award [1 max] for NO2 (g) + O (g) → NO (g) + O2 (g).

meta.title: Dinitrogen monoxide in the stratosphere is converted to nitrogen monoxide, NO (g). Write two equations to show how NO (g) catalyses the decomposition of ozone.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.4.2—Change in Gibbs energy, ΔG, relates the energy that can be obtained from a chemical reaction to the change in enthalpy, ΔH, change in entropy, ΔS, and absolute temperature, T. Apply the equation ΔG⦵ = ΔH⦵ − TΔS⦵ to calculate unknown values of these terms.

Reactivity 1.4.3—At constant pressure, a change is spontaneous if the change in Gibbs energy, ΔG, is negative. Interpret the sign of ΔG calculated from thermodynamic data. Determine the temperature at which a reaction becomes spontaneous.
